    • When jolt is applied via an Arc Soul, there is now a four-second internal cooldown for applying jolt again via this construct.

Strand

  • Grapple Tangles
    • Fixed an issue where players who didn’t have the Grapple Grenade equipped could Grapple Punch from a free grapple.

Titan

Sunbreaker

  • NEW: Shieldburst (Aspect)
    • Enhances your equipped barricade with Solar capabilities.
      • Towering Barricade: Your barricade slides forward across the ground before stopping, allowing you to advance in safety.
      • Rally Barricade: Standing behind your barricade grants scorching rounds to your allies’ Solar and Kinetic weapons.
    • While your barricade is deployed, activate your class ability again to remotely detonate it, scorching combatants caught in the detonation. Dealing damage increases the power of the blast.
  • Hammer Strike (Melee)
    • Increased damage versus combatants by 100%.

Sentinel

  • Ward of Dawn (Super)
    • Ability rework: Ward of Dawn now creates a much larger dome that you can fight inside.
    • Ward of Dawn now has a core object that destroys the ward if it’s destroyed.
      • The casting Titan can pick up the core object and throw it to relocate the Ward up to two times.
    • Allies inside the dome now gain Weapons of Light.
    • The dome’s owner now gains increased melee damage.
    • Enemies inside the dome are now weakened.
    • On deployment, now pulls nearby enemies into the dome.
    • Minor combatants inside the dome are now taunted by the players inside.
    • The Ward now persists after the casting Titan dies.
  • Offensive Bulwark (Aspect)
    • While the player has a Void Overshield, final blows now grant melee ability energy.
      • The grenade energy generation has also been rolled into this and is now granted on final blow instead of as increased recharge rate.
    • Unbreakable (Aspect)
      • Final blows with Unbreakable’s blast now grant Devour.
      • Slightly increased the size of Unbreakable’s blast.
    • Bastion (Aspect)
      • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.
    • Shield Bash (Melee)
      • Increased damage vsersus combatants by 100%.

Striker

  • Thruster (Class Ability)
    • Can now be activated while airborne.
  • Knockout (Aspect)
    • No longer grants a lunge-range increase vs. enemy players.
  • Touch of Thunder (Aspect)
    • Lightning Grenade
      • Now grants five stacks of Bolt Charge per hit.
    • Flashbang Grenade
      • The blinding pulse on first bounce now deals damage.
    • Storm Grenade
      • Increased storm duration by 0.5 seconds.
      • Increased maximum tracking speed versus combatants by 38%.
      • Increased time to reach max tracking speed by 25%.
    • Seismic Strike (Melee)
      • Increased damage versus combatants by 100%.
    • Knockout
      • Corrected an issue where the bonus damage could be applied too early, increasing the damage of the melee that activated it and causing unexpected one hit kills in Crucible.

 Behemoth

  • Tectonic Harvest (Aspect)
    • All Stasis subclasses now create Shatter Shards intrinsically.
    • Glacial Harvest gives Frost Armor when you pick up a Shatter Shard.
    • While you have Frost Armor, defeating an enemy with a Stasis or Kinetic weapon has a chance to shatter the enemy, dealing damage around the shattered enemy.
      • This chance increases based on how many Frost Armor stacks you have.
    • While you have Frost Armor, taking damage now grants grenade energy.
    • Decreased Fragment slots from three to two.
  • Diamond Lance (Aspect)
    • Decreased size of freeze detonation from thrown Diamond Lance versus players.
  • Cryoclasm (Aspect)
    • Sliding while you have Frost Armor now launches a Stasis wave that shatters Stasis Crystals and frozen enemies in front of you.
  • Glacial Quake (Super)
    • Heavy attacks now deal additional damage in an area in front of the Titan.

Berserker

  • Drengr’s Lash (Aspect)
    • Defeating elementally debuffed targets now grants Class Ability energy.
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.
    • The suspending wave’s impact damage now scales with your Class stat when your Class stat is above 100.

Hunter

Nightstalker

  • NEW: Phantom Surge (Melee)
    • Dash forward, slashing combatants as you pass through them. Deals significantly increased damage to Void-debuffed targets and targets marked by On The Prowl.
    • Generate Void Overshield for each combatant hit. Defeating at least one combatant grants additional Overshield and refunds some melee ability energy.
  • Trapper’s Ambush (Aspect)
    • Now deals significant area damage on impact instead of creating a smoke cloud.
      • Damage and radius is increased if cast while you have a Void buff.
    • Now uses class-ability energy instead of melee energy, and counts as a class ability for all perks and abilities.
    • If cast while you also have Vanishing Step equipped, you create a smoke cloud on landing (previous behavior).
    • Damage now scales with your Class stat when your Class stat is above 100.
  • On the Prowl (Aspect)
    • In addition to acquiring a target when you become invisible, can now also passively acquire a target after a short time if you’re not invisible.
  • Smoke Bomb (Melee)
    • Now natively grants invisibility to yourself and allies on deployment.
  • Mobius Quiver (Super)
    • Reduced maximum Moebius Quiver buff to Crossbow weapons by 50%.

Gunslinger

  • NEW: Crackshot (Aspect)
    • Activate your class ability to fire up to three scorching shots at targets you’ve marked. Aim at targets to mark them.
    • Landing all three shots cures you.
  • Acrobat’s Dodge (Class Ability)
    • Now can be activated while airborne.
  • Proximity Explosive Knife (Melee)
    • Increased detonation size from 5 to 6 meters.
    • Increased detonation damage from 90 to 100.
  • On Your Mark (Aspect)
    • Now grants Solar Restoration x1 for 3 seconds after final blows at ten stacks.
    • Final blows with Red Death Reformed grant Cure 1 + Resto 1 for 3 sec.
  • Knock ’em Down (Aspect)
    • Now grants Cure on super activation.
  • Gunpowder Gamble (Aspect)
    • Fixed an issue where the Gunpowder Gamble projectile could remain in the Hunter’s hand after throwing it.
  • Blade Barrage (Super)
    • Increased knife projectile speed and tracking.
    • Increased knife detonation size from 3 to 4.25 meters.
    • Increased knife detonation damage by 10%.
    • Knife detonations now Scorch enemies.
  • Golden Gun: Deadshot (Super)
    • Reduced super-energy gain from player kills by 40%.

Arcstrider

  • Arc Staff (Super)
    • Now grants Bolt Charge stacks on kill.
  • Lethal Current (Aspect)
    • Now jolts and creates an aftershock when used with Tempest Strike.
    • Now can be activated while in Super.
    • Fixed an issue where Lethal Current would sometimes deactivate before providing the aftershock benefits.
  • Ascension (Aspect)
    • Ascension detonation damage now scales with your Class stat when your Class stat is above 100.
    • Now counts as a class ability for all perks and abilities.

Threadrunner

  • Rope Dart (Melee)
    • Successfully catching the rope dart now grants Woven Mail based on the number of targets it defeated.
  • Widow’s Silk (Aspect)
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.
    • Fixed a bug where Widow’s Silk was not granting an extra grapple charge when using Silkstrike.
  • Threaded Specter (Aspect)
    • The specter’s detonation damage now scales with your Class stat when your Class stat is above 100.
  • Ensnaring Slam
    • Now counts as a class ability for all perks and abilities.

Revenant

  • Grim Harvest (Aspect)
    • All Stasis subclasses now create Shatter Shards intrinsically.
    • Glacial Harvest gives Frost Armor when you pick up a Shatter Shard.
    • While you have Frost Armor, defeating an enemy with a Stasis or Kinetic weapon has a chance to shatter the enemy, dealing damage around the shattered enemy.
      • This chance increases based on how many Frost Armor stacks you have.
    • While you have Frost Armor, taking damage now grants class-ability energy.
    • Decreased Fragment slots from three to two.
  • Shatterdive (Aspect)
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.
  • Winter’s Shroud (Aspect)
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.

Prismatic

  • Ascension (Aspect)
    • Can now activate the Winter’s Shroud Aspect on cast.

Warlock

Voidwalker

  • NEW: Soul Siphon (Aspect)
    • Ready this aspect by striking a foe with your powered melee ability.
    • Once readied, press your melee ability to begin siphoning energy from all enemies in front of you, dealing damage and gathering Overshield and class ability energy from each target.
  • Child of the Old Gods (Aspect)
    • Now suppresses combatants for a short time after initial attach.
    • Increased the Child’s max speed and acceleration by 100%.
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.
    • Fixed an issue where defeating enemies who were being drained by the Child with weapons would not provide class-ability energy.
  • Nova Bomb: Cataclysm (Super)
    • Allied projectiles now pass through the Nova Bomb projectile instead of colliding with it.

Dawnblade

  • Heat Rises (Aspect)
    • While Heat Rises is active, now reduces the accuracy of combatant attacks versus the Warlock.
  • Hellion (Aspect)
    • Reduced maximum target acquisition range from 40 to 32 meters.
  • Icarus Dash (Aspect)
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.

Stormcaller

  • Chain Lightning (Melee)
    • Increased damage vs. combatants by 100%.
    • Now has two ability charges by default.
  • Arc Soul (Aspect)
    • Reduced maximum target acquisition range from 30 to 24 meters.
  • Lightning Surge (Aspect)
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.

Shadebinder

  • Glacial Harvest (Aspect)
    • All Stasis subclasses now create Shatter Shards intrinsically.
    • Glacial Harvest gives Frost Armor when you pick up a Shatter Shard.
    • While you have Frost Armor, defeating an enemy with a Stasis or Kinetic weapon has a chance to shatter the enemy, dealing damage around the shattered enemy.
    • While you have Frost Armor, taking damage now grants grenade energy.
    • Decreased Fragment slots from three to two.
  • Frostpulse (Aspect)
    • Now grants five stacks of Frost Armor if this ability freezes a target.
    • Increased Fragment slots from two to three.
  • Winter’s Wrath (Super)
    • Winter’s Wrath projectiles now prioritize targets in alignment with their trajectory over closer targets.

Broodweaver

  • Mindspun Invocation (Aspect)
    • Threadling Grenade no longer creates perched Threadlings when consumed. Instead, Threadling Grenade can now be charged and thrown, spawning a Threadling nest (similar to Service of Luzaku) on impact.
  • The Wanderer (Aspect)
    • Threadling final blows now reduce your Tangle cooldown by one second.
  • Weavewalk (Aspect)
    • Melee attacks can no longer interrupt the Weavewalk exit animation.
  • Needlestorm (Super)
    • Fixed an issue where Needlestorm projectiles would not track certain bosses.

Healing Rift and Empowering Rift

  • Fixed an issue where Warlock Rifts could spawn under the ground if the player was throttling towards certain environmental objects.

Artifacts 2.0

  • Introducing Artifacts 2.0:
    • Simplified and streamlined how artifact perks are chosen.
    • Brought back a selection of Artifacts from previous Seasons, all of which have been updated to the Artifacts 2.0 system.
      • Players will have access to a total of seven Artifacts.
    • Removed Anti-Champion mods from all available Artifacts, as this functionality has been moved to being intrinsic to all weapons.
      • See our Anti-Champion 2.0 section.
    • Artifact mods are now disabled in competitive Crucible modes. This includes both the Glory playlist and Trials matches.
    • Removed New Gear bonus. Previous damage and DR increases have been folded into the base behavior for weapons and armor.
    • Fixed an issue where Seasonal Artifacts in Collections with visual stages did not display as if they had been fully unlocked.
    • Fixed an issue where some Seasonal Artifacts in Collections appeared as your equipped Ghost.

Queensfoil Censer

  • Torch
    • No longer increases damage versus bosses.
  • Heart of the Flame
    • Reduced top end to 15% increased damage for Supers based on nearby allies from 21%.
  • Bring into Being
    • Set equal to wearing three pieces of old season armor, perk can activate if the user has 60% Super energy or more.
  • Dragons Bite
    • Set to the value of wearing three pieces of armor, every three kills.
  • Creeping Chill
    • Now works with any Stasis defeat instead of just weapons. Also grants Frost Armor to allies near the explosion radius.
  • Solo Operative
    • Updated to be “precision final blows grant a scaling damage benefit with weapons until the user dies.”

Slayer Baron Apothecary Satchel

  • Wind Chill
    • Precision damage is no longer required to activate this perk.
  • Debilitating Wave
    • Significantly increased the tracking capabilities of the wave.
  • Curative Orbs
    • This perk no longer activates when picking up a Tangle but instead when destroying a Tangle (either by shooting it or upon impact after throwing it).
  • Void Renewal
    • The benefits from the old artifact perk Frenzied Hunger are now also granted upon collecting a Void Breach. Slightly increased these benefits to match the new activation requirement.
  • Kinetic Impacts
    • Special and Primary ammo Grenade Launchers can now also trigger this perk. The shockwave from Special and Primary ammo Grenade Launchers deals less damage than from Heavy Grenade Launchers.
    • The benefits from the old artifact perk ‘Rapid Impacts’ has been rolled into this perk.
  • Old God’s Rite
    • Increased the base damage of the projectiles by roughly 200%. In addition to this, the projectiles deal 85% more damage to orange bars, and 200% more damage to red bars.
  • Concussive Reload
    • This has been replaced with Weakened Clear. Armor of Eramis has been replaced with Frost Renewal.
  • Finders Keepers
    • This has been replaced with Killing Breeze.
  • Killing Breeze
    • Increased the duration from five to seven seconds.
  • The boost and/or seasonal gear bonus on these perks has been rolled into the base perk.
    • Wind Chill
    • Crystalline Converter
    • Total Carnage
    • Power From Pain
    • Trace Evidence
    • The Thick of It
    • Hail the Storm
    • Debilitating Wave
    • Retinal Burn
    • Curative Orbs
    • Void Renewal
    • Old God’s Rite
  • The boost and/or seasonal gear bonus on these perks has been removed.
    • Killing Breeze
    • Wind Chill
    • Brain Freeze
    • Conductive Cosmic Crystal
  • Fixed an issue where Crystalline Converter’s HUD buff would appear while at zero perk progress.

Hunters Journal

  • Sword Stamina
    • Decreased ammo granted to special swords on three rapid kills from three ammo to one ammo.
  • Sustained Fire
    • Decreased from Resist x3 to Resist x2.
  • Sustained Fire
    • Now works with Support Auto Rifles.
  • Shieldcrush
    • Updated to work with the Melee and Grenade stats.
  • Transference
    • Decreased number of kills needed for the max payout from 20 to 12.

Tablet of Ruin

  • Volatile Marksman
    • Boost portion ICD has been removed.
  • Elemental Siphon
    • Increased Super energy for picking up a matching pickup from 3% to 5%.
  • Vile Weave
    • Increased how many seconds are removed from Tangle cooldown from one to four seconds.
  • Flashover
    • Damage bonus reduced from 150% to 50%.
  • No Bell
    • Updated to be additive melee stacking at 85% (former high end), increased duration by 0.5 seconds.
  • Heavy Ordinance Regeneration
    • Increased benefits timer from nine to ten seconds.
  • Limit Break
    • Decreased benefit from 30% to 15%.
  • Gold from Lead
    • Decreased proc rate by ~50%.
  • Particle Reconstruction
    • Increased the minimum shots to proc by ~70%.

Implement of Curiosity

  • Pack Tactics
    • Increased maximum duration from seven to ten seconds.
  • Elemental Daze
    • Decreased damage of the blast by 25%.
  • Elemental Coalescence
    • Increased chance of creating the elemental pickup by 20%.
  • Energy Acceleration
    • Updated string to reference behavior vs Unstoppable Champions.
  • Solar Shrapnel
    • Updated spawning behavior to match Horde Shuttle. This will increase its potential in roaming content and reduce it versus bosses.
  • Elemental Overdrive
    • Increased boost from 10% to 22% to match 3x surge.
  • Iron Lord’s Vigor
    • Decreased ammo granted to special swords on three rapid kills from three ammo to one ammo.

Encrypted Data Disc

  • Kinetic Ammo Synthesis
    • Decreased ammo generation for Heavy weapons and Praxic Blade by 50%.
  • Payday
    • Decreased Super energy generation by 5%.
  • Snipers Meditation
    • Decreased the damage bonus by 5%.

NPA Repulsion Regulator

  • Improved Unraveling
    • Increased PvE damage bonus from 10% to 15%.
  • Bricks from Beyond
    • Now grants Heavy ammo bar progress instead of bricks.
  • Supernova
    • PvE weaken duration increased from six to eight seconds.
  • Lightning Strikes Twice
    • Now works with the Grenade stat.

Power and Progression

  • Power caps will not be increasing and Power level will not be reset.
    • The hard cap remains 550 Power and all your items will maintain their current Power levels.
  • Players will continue to find new rewards at minimum 300 Power, maintaining a change made in Renegades.
  • Portal Activity Reward Power
    • The Power of Portal Activity rewards are no longer capped per the difficulty level selected.
      • Earning an “A” grade (for example) will drop rewards at the same Power level, regardless of which difficulty you played.
    • The Power of Portal Activity rewards has been increased, allowing for faster progression.
      • “A” grades or higher will now continue to award positive Power progression all the way to the hard cap of 550.
      • Higher grades will continue to provide larger Power bonuses.
    • Miscellaneous rewards from sources throughout the game (such as pre-The Edge of Fate content) have been buffed.
      • These rewards will no longer be capped at 300 Power.
      • These rewards now have a chance to drop +1 Power up to 400 Power.
      • After 400 Power, these rewards will drop close to, but below, your current Power level.
    • Rewards purchased from legacy vendors have been buffed.
      • These rewards will no longer be capped at 300 Power.
      • These rewards will drop close to, but below, your current Power level.
    • Rewards pulled from Collections have been buffed to drop at 300 Power (up from 200).

Currencies and Materials

  • Updated max stack size limits on the following currencies:
    • Strange Coins
      • New Limit: 777
    • Enhancement Cores
      • New Limit: 9999
    • Enhancement Prisms
      • New Limit: 200
    • Ascendant Shard
      • New Limit: 50
    • Ascendant Alloy
      • New Limit: 50
    • Raid Banner
      • New Limit: 999
    • Fixed incorrectly assigned Collections reacquisition costs for several Ghost Shells, Ships, and Sparrows released since Episode: Revenant.

Experience

  • The weekly rest bonus remains available for the first 250K XP earned each week.
  • The weekly catch-up bonus introduced for the Lawless Rewards Pass has been removed. This bonus had further increased bonus XP each week to help keep Pass progression on track ahead of a Pass’ expiration date. Since the Lawless Rewards Pass has ended and the Triumphant Rewards Pass will not expire, this bonus has been retired.

Bright Dust

  • Increased Bright Dust gains across almost all weekly sources. On an annual basis, these increased earn rates will offset the loss of Bright Dust from new Rewards Passes and Seasonal Events.
    • Daily Objectives: 20 each (up from 10)
    • Weekly Objectives: 100 each (up from 50)
    • Weekly Hub Bonus: 20 (up from 10)
    • Weekly Reward Track: 1000 total (up from 650)
    • Active Orders (before Weekly Softcap): 1500 total (up from 1000)
      • 10 per Normal, 100 per Legendary, 200 per Exotic. These rates are unchanged.
    • Active Orders (after Weekly Softcap): Uncapped
      • 10 per 10 Normal, 20 per Legendary, 30 per Exotic. Legendary Order earn rate has been doubled.
    • Vanguard Alerts: 250 each (up from 50)
      • This can be earned twice a week.
    • Increased total Bright Dust available on the Triumphant Rewards Pass. See the Rewards Pass section below for more details.

Chronologs

General Updates

  • Chronologs will now be one of the four featured currencies shown across the UI, listed after Glimmer and before Silver.
  • Commander Zavala’s offer to acquire the previous week’s Vanguard Arsenal Credits will no longer cost Bright Dust. It will instead cost up to 200 Chronologs:
    • 100 Chronologs awards the first Arsenal Credit, unlocking three weapon choices
    • The fourth and fifth weapon choices can be unlocked for 50 Chronologs each.
  • As before, offers will still only be displayed for Arsenal Credits that were not earned from the Seasonal Hub’s Weekly Reward Track in the previous week.
  • Increased Chronolog gains across all Weekly sources.
    • Weekly Reward Track: 300 total (Up from 50).
    • Vanguard Alerts: 200 total (Up from 50).
      • This can be earned once a week.
    • Additionally, 3500 Chronologs will be available on the Triumphant Rewards Pass.

Vendors

Ada-1

  • Added additional shaders to Ada-1’s shader inventory:
    • Year 1 Shader Pack Shaders
    • Four Unreleased Shaders:
      • Alchemy Scorch
      • Bonemeal Loam
      • Brineglow
      • Charred Plumage
    • Increased number of available shaders per week from three to six.
      • Still randomly rotates through available shaders each week.
    • Updated Ada-1’s Legacy Armor to no longer be on a rotation. Expanded selection to all armors for transmog purposes.
    • Updated Threader Bounty Reset:
      • No longer resets seasonally, instead players will earn one additional threader bounty, per class, per week.
        • New bounties available each week at weekly reset.
        • Maximum number of available Threader Bounties, per class, is ten.

Drifter

  • Gambit Reputation Rank Reward Track has been restored.
  • Updated Gambit Focusing:
    • Gear additions
    • Tired focusing will utilize Gambit engrams.
    • Now includes focusing for all tiered Gambit gear.

Lord Shaxx

  • Crucible Reputation Rank Reward Track has been restored.
  • Updated Crucible Focusing:
    • Now includes focusing for all tiered Crucible and Competitive Crucible gear.
      • Gear additions
      • Includes Tiered gear from the Edge of Fate, Ash & Iron, and Renegades.
      • Tiered Focusing will utilize Crucible engrams.
        • Competitive Crucible gear will require Crucible Ciphers which can be obtained via Competitive Crucible.

Commander Zavala

  • Vanguard Reputation Rank Reward Track has been restored.
  • Updated Vanguard Focusing:
    • Now includes focusing for all tiered Solo Ops, Fireteam Ops, Arena Ops and Pinnacle Ops gear.
      • Gear additions
      • Includes Tiered gear from the Edge of Fate, Ash & Iron, and Renegades.
      • Tiered Focusing will utilize Vanguard engrams.
        • Pinnacle Ops and Arena Ops gear will require Vanguard Ciphers which can be obtained via Pinnacle Ops and Arena Ops activities.
          • Nightfall Ciphers that players may have in their inventory will be automatically updated to Vanguard Ciphers.
        • Rewards from Call to Arms are available to pick up from Zavala.
        • Updated the rotation for Weekly Vanguard Arms Rewards.

Saint-14

  • Trials of Osiris Reputation Rank Reward Track has been restored.
  • Updated Trials Focusing:
    • Now includes focusing for all tiered Trials gear.
      • Gear additions
      • Includes Tiered gear from the Edge of Fate, Ash & Iron, and Renegades.
      • Focusing will utilize Trials engrams.

Saladin

  • Updated Iron Banner Focusing:
    • Now includes focusing for all tiered Iron Banner gear.
      • Gear additions
      • Includes Tiered gear from the Edge of Fate, Ash & Iron, and Renegades.
      • Focusing will utilize Iron Banner Ciphers, obtained through Iron Banner match completions.
    • Iron Banner will maintain Event Card, and will not see it’s Reputation Rank Reward track restored.

Banshee-44

  • Gunsmith Reputation Rank Reward Track has been restored.
    • Dismantling weapons and armors gives Gunsmith Reputation again.
  • Updated Gunsmith Focusing:
    • Now includes Shared Pool weapons from previous releases.
  • Includes Tiered gear from the Edge of Fate, Ash & Iron, and Renegades.

Hawthorne

  • Updated Clan engrams to give tiered gear.

Xûr

  • Xûr’s Reputation Rank Reward Track has been restored.
    • Purchasing items from Xur grants reputation.
  • Xûr’s Inventory has been updated:
    • New Catalysts
    • Tiered Armor

Lodi

  • Lodi is now in the Tower. You can find him enjoying his coffee in the Bazaar near Ikora, upstairs in New Monarchy’s old space.

Identity and Expression

Expanded Ornaments for Exotic Armor

  • All qualities of Armor Ornament from Common to Legendary can now be applied to the currently equipped Exotic armor alongside Universal Exotic Ornaments.
    • If the ornament is not specifically parented to the equipped Exotic armor, its visuals will be temporarily disabled in PvP content.
    • Special visual cues or effects applied by ornaments that are not specifically parented to the equipped Exotic armor may not be visible.
  • Exotic Ornaments have been added to Collections.

General Fixes

  • The Death+∞ ornament for Suros Regime will now appear in Collections.
  • The icon for the Reefmade shader has been updated to better communicate its purple themes.
  • The tooltips for the Singular Mercenary, Devoted Warrior, and Noble Partisan armor ornament sets will now correctly note these are associated with a partnership.
  • The Polar Wolf helm for Hunters inadvertently shipped without its ears. We have added a second version of the ornament that includes the ears while leaving the original intact. Existing and future owners of the Hunter version of the Polar Wolf Set can claim this additional version from the Special Deliveries kiosk in the Tower.
  • The Positive Practice emote inadvertently shipped without its intended audio. This has been added to the emote. We’re investigating adding an alternate version of this emote without its audio for those who may prefer that version.
  • Updated the Chlorophyta Mask/Helm/Hood from the Reclamation Rewards Pass to reduce a persistent yellow effect, better aligning its visuals with the Chlorophyta Armor Set.

General

Product Updates

  • The Year of Prophecy Deluxe Edition and the Year of Prophecy Ultimate Edition have been retired.
    • The New Land Beyond Exotic and its catalyst will now be available to new and existing owners of either The Edge of Fate Standard Edition or Renegades Standard Edition. These can be claimed from the Special Deliveries Kiosk in the Tower.
      • New Land Beyond will also be available on the Monument of Lost Lights for owners of either expansion.
    • The Year of Prophecy Ultimate Edition’s themed armor ornament sets will now be available on the Eververse Partner tab for those who do not own the Year of Prophecy Ultimate Edition. Owners of the Year of Prophecy Ultimate Edition can continue to claim their ornaments from the Special Deliveries kiosk if they have not already.
  • The fourth Rewards Pass for the Year of Prophecy – included in the Deluxe and Ultimate Editions – has been renamed from Shadow & Order Rewards Pass to Triumphant Rewards Pass. As the final Rewards Pass in Destiny 2, the Triumphant Rewards Pass has no pre-planned expiration date.
  • All owners of The Edge of Fate: Standard Edition who had purchased prior to the release of Renegades will now retroactively receive both the Reclamation Rewards Pass and the Ash & Iron Rewards Pass if unowned.
    • All purchases of The Edge of Fate: Standard Edition since Renegades launched already provided both passes. This remains unchanged.
  • All owners of Renegades: Standard Edition will now receive both the Lawless Rewards Pass and the Triumphant Rewards Pass as part of their purchase.
    • This change applies retroactively to existing owners of Renegades Standard Edition. Existing owners will automatically receive access to the Triumphant Rewards Pass upon login to Monument of Triumph.
    • For new and future purchasers of Renegades Standard Edition, the Lawless Rewards Pass will be unlocked on the Monument to Seasons Past.
      • If the account played during Lawless and acquired enough progress to earn ranks, some rewards will be ready to claim.
      • Any remaining items can be acquired for Chronologs like any other expired Pass.
    • Destiny 2: The Collection is now available on all platforms. This collection offers all Standard Editions and Legacy Packs for each expansion in one product, along with the Dungeon Keys for The Witch Queen, Lightfall, and The Final Shape and the four Reward Passes from Year of Prophecy.
      • Dungeon Keys remain available in the Eververse individually or in a pack. These will show as Owned if the Keys are acquired from Destiny 2: The Collection.
    • Adjusted the brightness of the Fate Saga backgrounds on the Timeline to improve readability on a wider range of screen types.
    • Access to The Edge of Fate and Renegades campaigns has been moved to the revamped Destinations screen.
    • The Timeline icon has been updated on the revamped Destinations screen.
    • A Timeline node has been added for the Monument of Triumph era.

Destiny Announcements

  • The Destiny Announcements screen has been added to orbit. This screen houses up to thirty-six of the most recent messages shared by the Destiny team.
    • This will allow you to refer to announcements that you may want or need to re-read, may have missed, or had accidentally dismissed for up to thirty days after receipt, after which they will naturally expire and be removed.
  • The system will now support automatic delivery of messages to the Announcement screen, resulting in fewer pop-up messages overall.
  • The Announcements screen also includes a Featured Item carousel including standing messages and information about relevant content.

Rewards Pass Updates

General

  • The expiration timer has been removed.
  • The repeatable 5-Rank and 10-Rank offers in the Eververse have been retired.

Rewards Track

  • The Triumphant Rewards Pass has received additional cosmetic rewards.
  • The total Bright Dust awarded on the Triumphant Rewards Pass has increased from 10,500 to 14,000 and is free to earn for all players.
  • The first rank of the Free Track on the Triumphant Rewards Pass will award an Eververse Engram.
  • The Score Boost for Portal Activities has been replaced with a Minor XP Boost.
    • The Minor XP Boost provides +3% Personal and Fireteam XP for each level unlocked, up to a maximum of five levels and +15%.
    • This Boost honors Score Boost progression from the Lawless Rewards Pass, with your total Score Boost carried over and converted to the Minor XP Boost.
    • Once an account has earned five levels of the Minor XP Boost across both the Lawless Rewards Pass and the Triumphant Rewards Pass, the Minor XP Boost will be capped at 15%.
      • Any remaining Ranks on the Triumphant Reward Pass that would provide a Minor XP Boost will instead award ten Enhancement Cores.

Monument to Seasons Past Updates

General

  • The Ash & Iron Rewards Pass and the Lawless Rewards Pass have been added to the Monument to Seasons Past.
  • The seasonal armor bundles for the Year of The Final Shape have been added to their relevant Episodes.

Exchange

  • The Chronolog Exchange has been updated to support a weekly or bi-weekly purchase cycle for every resource. Below we’ve listed the items offered with their individual Chronolog (CL) cost and how many can be purchased per-week:
    • Enhancement Prism: 50 Chronologs, 10x per week.
    • Ascendant Alloy: 200 Chronologs, 1x per week.
    • Ascendant Shard: 300 Chronologs, 1x per week.
    • Deepsight Harmonizer: 300 Chronologs, 1x per two weeks.
    • 7x Strange Coins: 50 Chronologs, 10x per week.
    • Synthweave Template: 500 Chronologs, 1x per two weeks.
    • Exotic Cipher: 500 Chronologs, 1x per week.
    • Bright Engram: 200 Chronologs, 2x per week.
    • Exotic Engram: 300 Chronologs, 1x per week.

Evergreen Eververse

General Updates

  • Silver and Bright Dust will continue to be relevant currencies in Destiny 2 – any reserves will retain their value.
  • The Recommendations System has been retired.
  • Ornament Sales from the Appearance screen will feature both Silver and Bright Dust offers from The Witch Queen to today.
    • Older items can be acquired from Daily Offers or Bright Engram Focusing in the Eververse.
  • The final slate of new Eververse items will be listed on the Featured Tab through June 23, and on the Seasonal Offerings tab thereafter.
  • The final Foundation event in the Eververse will feature the new Vivid Spectrum Holochip alongside a rerun of last year’s Pride Shaders through June 23.

Daily Offers

  • The Daily Offers tab offers a different selection of Bright Dust and Silver offers each day, drawn from across the history of Destiny 2, including Monument of Triumph.
  • While all players will see the same slate of offers each day, each offer slot is like a deck of cards: any item appearing in a slot will always appear in the same slot again at a future date, but each item in that slot’s deck must appear once before the slot will reshuffle its contents and start again.
  • Daily Offer categories include:
    • For Bright Dust: an Exotic Weapon Ornament slot, an Armor Ornament slot, an Exotic emote slot, a slot for each major Exotic accessory (Ghost Shell, Ship, and Vehicle), two emote slots, a Legendary Ghost Projection Slot, four Legendary Shader slots, and three Legendary Transmat Effect slots
      • The Armor Ornament slot includes both Exotic Armor Ornaments and Legendary Armor Augments that pair with an Exotic armor for the current class, but not individual parts of Legendary Armor Ornament Sets. These can instead be acquired as packs or from Focused Engrams.
      • The Vehicle slot includes both Sparrows and Skimmers.
      • The Emote slots include both Legendary and Rare emotes.
    • For Silver: two Exotic emote slots, one slot for each major Exotic Accessory (Ghost Shell, Ship, and Vehicle), and three slots for Legendary Emotes and Finishers.
      • The Vehicle slot includes both Sparrows and Skimmers.
    • No items are shared between slots of the same currency and type. For example, the Grillmaster Ghost Shell can appear for Silver or Bright Dust, but the Occultation Shader will only appear in one of the four Shader Bright Dust slots.
      • The same item can appear for Silver and Bright Dust in the same day, just like two independent decks of cards can both have the same card next in line at the same time.
    • Daily Offers will host a selection of items – specifically Ghost Shells, Ships, Sparrows, Skimmers, and an emote – that are uniquely available only through Daily Offers or Bright Engram Focusing.
    • After June 23, the Featured Daily Offers tab will replace Featured Items as the Eververse’s landing screen. This tab will feature the Daily Silver Offers, an offer for a single Synthweave Template, and a link to the full Daily Offers tab.

Legends

  • The Legends tab has been refreshed to house content from our Featured Partners.
    • Items on this tab will be available for as long as Destiny 2 is permitted to offer them, after which they will be retired.
    • Partner items will only be available for Silver and will not be available outside of this tab.
    • When all items in the Legends tab are eventually retired, this tab will automatically be removed from the Eververse.

Seasonal Offerings / Archive

  • Seasonal Offerings will permanently house Armor Ornament Set Packs, Weapon Ornament Packs, Shader Packs, Skimmer Packs, and Holochips added with Monument of Triumph, as well as Monument of Triumph’s new Weapon Ornaments, Exotic Armor Ornaments, and Legendary Augments.
  • The Archive will house Armor Ornament Set Packs, Weapon Ornament Packs, Shader Packs, Skimmer Packs, and Holochips offered prior to Monument of Triumph.
    • This includes all previous Holiday Event armors, available for both Silver and Bright Dust.
  • Except for the free Runner Kit, the full contents of each pack in Seasonal Offerings or the Archive – including every Silver-only Armor Ornament Set and new item added in Monument of Triumph – can also be acquired through Focused Bright Engrams.
    • Any components of a pack that match the category of a Daily Offer slot, like a Weapon Ornament, will eventually appear in Daily Offers as well.
  • Filtering Options have been adjusted for the new functionalities.

Bright Engram Focusing

  • Added Bright Engram Focusing to the Eververse.
  • A Focused Engram includes items released through Monument of Triumph, even some items previously unavailable for Bright Dust, and will award an unowned item of the Engram’s category.
  • Several Focused Engrams have more items in them than can be shown in the preview of their contents, but will not drop duplicates, and their contents are finite.
    • When a Focused Engram has been completely emptied, its preview will be empty and additional purchases of that Engram will be blocked.
  • Bright Engram Focusing costs per category are:
For one (1) Eververse Engram +
0 Bright Dust · Standard Decryption with no focusing
300 Bright Dust · Legendary Accessories – Ghosts, Ships, and Vehicles together

· Legendary Shaders

· Legendary Flair – Holochips, Ghost Holograms, and Transmat Effects together.
600 Bright Dust · Exotic Accessories – Ghosts, Ships, Vehicles individually, with Skimmers included in Vehicles.

· Legendary Emotes – Single-Player & Multiplayer together

· Legendary Finishers
900 Bright Dust · Exotic Weapon Ornaments

· Exotic Emotes – Single-Player & Multiplayer together

· Legendary & Exotic Armor Ornaments – individual engrams per class and slot combination.

Legacy Engrams

  • Bright Engram Focusing will also provide a one-to-one exchange for Legacy Engrams if present in your Engram inventory. Eligible legacy engrams include:
S1: Bright Engram S4: Steadfast Engram S6: Jubilant Engram
S2: Illuminated Engram S4: Ephemeral Engram S7: Best of Year 1 Engram
S2: Dawning Engram S5: Etched Engram S8: Nostalgic Engram
S2: Crimson Engram S5: Winterdrift Engram S9: Fond Memories Engram
S3: Prototype Engram S5: Crimson Engram S10: Luminous Engram
S3: Solstice Engram S6: Notorious Engram S11: Mnemonic Engram
